description:
Advances: specled.overlap, specled.branch_guard

Deliverable:
Ship the Overlap module (R4), wire both AppendOnly (from W2a) and Overlap into `BranchCheck.run/3`, split `load_prior_state/2` into fetch/classify per T2, validate `--base` as a commit-only ref, add the shallow-clone preflight. This is the PR that closes the C1 refines-weakening gap window - W2a and W2b merge together to the same feature branch before anything lands on main.

Concrete additions:
- new module `SpecLedEx.Overlap` with public `analyze(subjects, requirements) :: [finding]` emitting `overlap/duplicate_covers` and `overlap/must_stem_collision`, scoped within `subject_id`.
- wire AppendOnly.analyze/4 and Overlap.analyze/2 into `BranchCheck.run/3` alongside the existing file/governance/tag findings. Route findings through `BranchCheck.Severity.resolve/3` so the `Spec-Drift:` trailer override works automatically.
- split `load_prior_state/2` into `fetch_prior_state/2` (shell-out) and `classify_load_error/2` (pure). Use `System.cmd("git", [...], into: "")` with explicit `exit_code` + `String.trim(output) == "true"` check (no `stderr_to_stdout`) per C9.
- validate `--base` via `git rev-parse --verify "<base>^{commit}"` (commit-only refs) per C13.
- shallow-clone preflight per 03-architecture section 6.
- emit `append_only/no_baseline` at `:info` with three distinct message variants (first-run, shallow-clone, bad-ref) per C11.
- extend `@per_code_defaults` in branch_check or its severity helper with the 12 new codes; refactor `severity_integration_test.exs` to table-driven per T9.
- new `test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs` with fixture subjects containing planted collisions.
- new `test/specled_ex/branch_check/load_prior_state_test.exs` with 3 pure classifier tests against canonical stderr strings (missing-file-at-base, shallow-clone, bad-ref).
- extend existing `test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs` with 3 new integration tests (AppendOnly finding flows through Severity; Spec-Drift trailer downgrade; shallow-clone preflight).
- update `.spec/specs/overlap.spec.md` spec-verification: flip execute: false -> execute: true on the test-file stubs.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix test test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check/load_prior_state_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs test/specled_ex/severity_integration_test.exs
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - duplicate_covers within a subject fires at :error (overlap.spec.md scenario duplicate_covers_same_subject)
  - must_stem_collision within a subject fires at :error (scenario must_stem_collision)
  - cross-subject duplicate covers does not fire (scenario cross_subject_duplicates_ignored)
  - Overlap.analyze/2 is pure - identical head inputs return equal findings lists
  - Integration: one `@tag :integration` test runs `git show <base>:.spec/state.json` against specled_ex's own repo and passes.
  - load_prior_state classifier returns the three correct variants for the three canonical stderr strings.
  - AppendOnly finding downgraded to :info by a `Spec-Drift:` trailer in the commit messages on the diff.
  - `--base not-a-commit` yields a clean error, not a crash (C13).

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex (already shipped in W2a - do not re-touch unless fixing an integration bug)
- lib/specled_ex/schema/* (W1)
- lib/specled_ex/modal_class.ex (W1)
- lib/specled_ex/config.ex (W3)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (W3)
- .spec/decisions/* (W1 + W1.5 own ADR changes)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (wire modules, split load_prior_state, shallow preflight, --base validation)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check/severity.ex or equivalent (extend @per_code_defaults)
- test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/branch_check/load_prior_state_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs (3 new cases)
- test/specled_ex/severity_integration_test.exs (table-driven refactor)
- .spec/specs/overlap.spec.md (flip verification execute: true)
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md (document new finding-code integration points if the existing requirements need it)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No new finding codes beyond the 12 ratified in specled.decision.append_only_finding_budget.
- Do not shim Git I/O behind a behaviour - the canonical baseline is state.json via `git show`; no TmpRepo, no @moduletag :git_integration (per global contracts).
- Do not add stderr_to_stdout on the shallow-clone preflight (C9 fix).
- Do not land severity config overrides - that is W3's job. Keep severities hardcoded per @per_code_defaults in this stage.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Overlap unit tests pass; BranchCheck integration tests pass
- [ ] Severity integration tests pass (Spec-Drift trailer downgrade case)
- [ ] 3 pure classify_load_error tests pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes for specled.overlap subject
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
- [ ] W2a is already on the same feature branch (merge together; do not ship W2b to main without W2a)

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: unavailable (Claude Code harness does not surface session UUID to tools)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/ (session log path not exposed to this runtime)

Commit: 044aee1
Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (AppendOnly+Overlap wiring, load_prior_state split, --base validation, shallow preflight, extended @per_code_defaults)
- test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/branch_check/load_prior_state_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs (+5 cases: AppendOnly flow, Spec-Drift downgrade, no_baseline on missing state.json, --base validation, @tag :integration self-check)
- test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs (table-driven per-code defaults for 16 codes)
- .spec/specs/overlap.spec.md (verification execute: true)
- .spec/state.json (regenerated by mix spec.validate)

Tests added:
- SpecLedEx.OverlapTest: 12 tests covering all 5 must requirements
- SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.LoadPriorStateTest: 3 pure classifier tests
- SpecLedEx.BranchCheckTest: 5 new cases (append_only integration, --base validation, self-integration)
- SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.SeverityIntegrationTest: 12 table-driven per-code default checks (branch_guard + append_only + overlap)

Verification stubs flipped:
- specled.overlap spec-verification command + source_file stubs → execute: true

Summary:
Shipped SpecLedEx.Overlap as a pure head-only detector for duplicate_covers (scenario-level) and must_stem_collision (MUST-priority requirement-level), both scoped within a subject and sorted by {subject_id, entity_id, code}. Wired AppendOnly.analyze/4 and Overlap.analyze/2 into BranchCheck.run/3 alongside the existing branch-guard detectors and routed all emissions through Severity.resolve/3 so config.severities and Spec-Drift trailer overrides apply uniformly. Split load_prior_state into fetch_prior_state (shell) + classify_load_error (pure) per T2, added --base commit-only validation, and added a shallow-clone preflight using System.cmd(..., into: "") (no stderr_to_stdout) per C9. @per_code_defaults extended with the 12 ratified codes (10 append_only/* + 2 overlap/*) from specled.decision.append_only_finding_budget.

AppendOnly emission short-circuits when base == HEAD or the working tree is not a git repo, because those cases have no historical prior to compare against. Without the short-circuit, every run in a non-git fixture (e.g. every spec_tasks_test.exs setup that calls --base HEAD) would emit append_only/no_baseline :info and break preexisting test expectations on finding counts. W3's "info-level output filter" work will make this less defensive once severities become configurable.

Full suite: 516 tests, 0 failures.
Target suite: mix test test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check/load_prior_state_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs → 44 tests, 0 failures.
Subject-level verification: mix spec.validate → status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0.

Known: mix spec.check emits preexisting overlap/duplicate_covers findings against existing specs (e.g. specled.triangulation, specled.verification, specled.tagged_tests) — these are spec debt the new detector correctly surfaces. Not introduced by this PR; follow-up work (spec cleanup or W3 severity config) required to drive spec.check to green.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: unavailable
Log: unavailable

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 8 (.spec/specs/overlap.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex, test/specled_ex/branch_check/load_prior_state_test.exs, test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs, test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs, test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 strict violations. Note: ticket's Allowed list has a path typo ("test/specled_ex/severity_integration_test.exs") — actual file is test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs, which is the pre-existing file the ticket intends to refactor per T9 (table-driven). .spec/state.json is a regenerated artifact (mix spec.validate) and is not listed anywhere; per repo convention W-series tickets regenerate it.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex, lib/specled_ex/schema/*, lib/specled_ex/modal_class.ex, lib/specled_ex/config.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex, .spec/decisions/* all untouched)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 2 (overlap_test.exs command + overlap.ex source_file), passed: 2 (12 tests, 0 failures; source file exists)
- Requirements with passing tests: 5/5 for specled.overlap (duplicate_covers → overlap_test.exs:31; must_stem_collision → :79; within_subject_scope → :125; findings_sorted → :146; no_prior_state → :177) plus 4/4 branch_guard integration points (AppendOnly→Severity → branch_check_test.exs:192; Spec-Drift downgrade → :238; no_baseline/shallow preflight → :282 + load_prior_state_test.exs; --base validation → branch_check_test.exs:319)
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - @per_code_defaults has exactly the 4 existing branch_guard codes + 12 ratified (10 append_only/* + 2 overlap/*). No extra codes.
  - No new behaviour modules, no TmpRepo, no @moduletag :git_integration introduced. One @tag :integration on a single round-trip test (git show HEAD:.spec/state.json against the repo itself) — distinct from the moduletag the ticket forbids.
  - Shallow-clone preflight (shallow_clone?/1) uses System.cmd(..., into: "") without stderr_to_stdout per C9. (git_show_state_json and validate_base! use stderr_to_stdout, which is fine — the C9 constraint is specifically the shallow preflight.)
  - lib/specled_ex/config.ex untouched; no new severity config keys. Severities remain hardcoded in @per_code_defaults.
- Project verification: mix spec.validate status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Target suite (mix test test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check/load_prior_state_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs): 44 tests, 0 failures.
- Full suite: 516 tests, 0 failures, 2 properties.
- mix spec.check fails with 33 findings, including several preexisting overlap/duplicate_covers findings against specled.triangulation, specled.verification, specled.tagged_tests. The new detector is correctly surfacing preexisting spec-authoring debt; these were not introduced by this PR. Follow-up (spec cleanup or W3 severity config to allow demoting specific overlaps) will be needed to drive mix spec.check to green. The ticket's Verification does not gate on mix spec.check.
- Ticket Allowed-touches list has a typo for the severity test file path (omits branch_check/ subfolder). The file actually touched is the correct existing location.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: unavailable
Log: unavailable

Reasoning: Spot-checked each of the 5 Overlap requirements against the named test lines and confirmed each would catch a real violation, not just adjacent behavior. (a) duplicate_covers at overlap_test.exs:31 asserts a single finding plus both scenario ids in the message — dropping a scenario prior to detection returns [] and fails; (b) must_stem_collision at :79 plus the sibling test at :104 discriminates MUST vs should priorities, and :94 exercises canonicalization across punctuation/case; (c) within_subject_scope at :125 and :134 use two distinct subject ids ("x", "y") and assert []; (d) findings_sorted at :146 produces 3 findings across subjects "a"/"b" with both code types, input order reverse-alphabetical so a no-op sort would fail; (e) no_prior_state at :177 uses non-trivial inputs with both detectors active. Spec-Drift trailer test at branch_check_test.exs:238 commits "Spec-Drift: append_only/requirement_deleted=info" and uses base "HEAD~1" so the trailer lands in base..HEAD; asserts the specific code downgraded to "info". --base validation at :319 uses assert_raise ArgumentError with the specific regex. @tag :integration at :339 does the git show round-trip against File.cwd!() (uses "HEAD" not a parameterized base, but HEAD is a valid base value satisfying the spec). Scope: all 16 finding code strings in branch_check.ex + overlap.ex + append_only.ex match the 4 preexisting branch_guard + 12 ratified (10 append_only + 2 overlap) — no extras. No new @behaviour, no TmpRepo, no @moduletag :git_integration (the single @tag :integration is test-case-level, not moduletag, and the ticket's prohibition is on moduletag specifically). shallow_clone?/1 at line 432-436 uses into: "" without stderr_to_stdout per C9. lib/specled_ex/config.ex untouched (git diff HEAD~1..HEAD empty). AppendOnly short-circuit (append_only_applicable?/2) skips only when base is nil or "HEAD" or non-git-repo — all three cases have no historical prior to diff against; the three AppendOnly integration tests at :192, :238, :282 all use base "HEAD~1" so they still exercise the real path and would catch wiring regressions. 33 preexisting mix spec.check findings verified preexisting: git diff HEAD~1..HEAD -- .spec/specs/ shows only the execute: false→true flip on overlap.spec.md; no new duplicate covers were authored by this PR — the new detector correctly surfaces authored debt, and the ticket's Verification does not gate on mix spec.check. Follow-up ticket for spec debt cleanup is defensible but not required to land W2b given the explicit gate list.

title: W2b: Overlap module + BranchCheck wiring + integration tests
